# `web_resize`

Resize the browser viewport to the given dimensions.

Use to test responsive CSS at different breakpoints (e.g., 375x812 for phone,
768x1024 for tablet). Does NOT change User-Agent or device emulation flags —
pages that UA-sniff still see desktop Chrome. For full mobile emulation set
the device's profile at creation time, not via this tool.

## Source

- Kind: class-backed
- Class: `xyz.block.trailblaze.playwright.tools.PlaywrightNativeResizeTool`

## Contract

- Visible to LLM: yes (`surface_to_llm: true`)
- Recordable: yes (`is_recordable: true`)
- Host-only: no (`requires_host: false`)

## Input schema

### Required parameters

- `width` — `Integer`
  Width of the viewport in CSS pixels. Must be positive.
- `height` — `Integer`
  Height of the viewport in CSS pixels. Must be positive.

### Optional parameters

- `reasoning` — `String`

## Output

Returns: `string` (opaque text content)

Typed result schemas (`kind: query | action`, MCP `structuredContent`) are not yet carried by the resolved manifest — this section will gain detail when that lands.
